evaluations.query: Audit which OrchestKit agents actually get spawned vs generic ones
expected_behavior: Reads .claude/logs/subagent-spawns.jsonl (the fresh stream), not the dead agent-usage.jsonl
Computes the generic-vs-ork-catalog-vs-other spawn split as counts and percentages
Lists agents that fired and agents that never fired in the window
Reports concentration (top-5 agents as a share of catalog spawns)

evaluations.query: Classify our dormant agents — prune, fix triggers, or niche?
expected_behavior: Classifies each dormant agent by its real skill-reference count, not description quality
Buckets agents into active, mis-triggered, niche, or dead per rules/activation-status.md
Marks an agent DEAD only when it has zero references in any skill or agent
For mis-triggered agents, recommends adding a subagent_type= spawn from a high-traffic skill

evaluations.query: Should we rewrite agent descriptions to fix low activation?
expected_behavior: Does NOT recommend rewriting descriptions to add 'use proactively'
Cites that an A/B test showed description rewrites give zero routing benefit
Redirects the fix to skill-wiring and telemetry
Notes the per-agent description byte budget constraint

evaluations.query: Run an activation health report before trimming the catalog
expected_behavior: Runs or references scripts/run-activation-audit.sh for the deterministic data
Caveats that never-fired means absent from the window, a strong signal but not proof of zero
